problem: What triggers cancer cells to leave primary tumor and colonize new tissue?
mechanism: Autophagy activation to clear misfolded proteins
hypothesis: What triggers cancer cells to leave primary tumor and colonize new tissue? remains unsolved because previous research missed the role of Autophagy. Autophagy activation to clear misfolded proteins directly addresses this.

experiment.protocol: In vitro cell culture assay with 32 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: We cannot solve problems with the same thinking that created them. — Einstein paraphrase
